text: What is Booz Allen's revenue growth rate over the last four quarters?
thesis: Booz Allen should outperform over the next 2–4 quarters if demand for AI/cybersecurity solutions within the intelligence community accelerates and contract award timelines normalize. The firm's high-margin consulting work, deep relationships with defense and civilian agencies, and recurring revenue from mission-critical systems provide earnings stability and margin expansion potential. The thesis fails if government shutdown risks materialize, procurement reforms compress billing rates, or talent retention costs spike

thesis: Delta’s EPS is convex to stable fuel prices and sustained load factors above 85%. The thesis fails if fuel prices rise sharply or ticket yield compresses.

text: How does JPMorgan's efficiency ratio compare to its peers in the banking sector?
thesis: JPMorgan should outperform over the next 4–6 quarters if net interest income remains resilient as the rate cycle peaks and credit quality holds up better than market fears. The bank's scale, diversified revenue streams (investment banking, markets, wealth management), and best-in-class efficiency provide multiple levers for earnings stability. The thesis fails if a hard landing accelerates loan loss provisions beyond consensus expectations or if investment banking recovery disappoints.

Mounjaro/Zepbound represent a multi-billion dollar franchise with pricing power and durable competitive moats, driving operating leverage in the P&L. The thesis fails if competitive entrants erode pricing faster than anticipated, clinical setbacks emerge in the pipeline, or reimbursement dynamics shift negatively.

The thesis fails if real yields remain elevated.

text: Is Air Products' capex as a percentage of revenue declining under the new CEO while ROIC is improving, and is this capital discipline translating into a narrowing ROIC gap versus Linde over the last 8 quarters?
thesis: Air Products should deliver stable defensive returns over 12-18 months anchored by long-term take-or-pay industrial gas contracts with recession-resistant demand. New CEO has imposed capital discipline with capped core capex, guiding high-single-digit EPS growth. The thesis fails if mega-project execution risk on hydrogen ventures materializes into writedowns.

text: Is GE Aerospace's commercial services margin expanding while defense segment margin is compressing, and what is the net blended margin trend over the last 6 quarters?
thesis: GE Aerospace should outperform over 12 months if LEAP engine aftermarket revenue accelerates as installed engines enter first major overhaul cycles, driving high-margin services growth. The large installed engine base generates the majority of commercial revenue from parts and services. The thesis fails if next-gen program losses for delayed Boeing widebody escalate or airframer delivery rates disappoint.

text: Is RCL's fuel cost per available passenger cruise day rising faster than its net yield per passenger day is growing, and is the spread between net yield growth and fuel cost growth narrowing quarter over quarter?
thesis: Royal Caribbean should outperform over 12-18 months if record cruise demand persists — the company reported its strongest-ever booking period with majority of next-year capacity booked at record rates. EPS guidance exceeds consensus reflecting strong pricing power. The thesis fails if macro slowdown curbs discretionary cruise spending or fuel spikes compress margins.
